constraints: Chef is watching the new season of Squid Game, where contestants face a glass bridge challenge.
The bridge has MMM steps, and each step consists of two glass panels suspended side by side.
**Exactly one** panel on each step is strong enough to support a contestant's weight, while the other panel will shatter, causing the contestant to fall and die.
There are NNN contestants who cross the bridge one by one, in sequence. Each contestant can observe the path taken by the previous contestant(s).
For each step i (1≤i≤M)i \ (1 \le i \le M)i (1≤i≤M), determine the probability that **all NNN contestants** die **before crossing** step iii.
The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of a single line of input comprising of two space-separated integers NNN and MMM denoting the number of contestants and the number of steps respectively.
For each test case, print MMM space-separated integers where ithi^{th}ith integer is probability of all NNN contestants dying before crossing step iii.

The probability can be written in the form of PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ such that QQQ is not divisible by 109+710^9 + 7109+7. Output the value of P⋅Q−1mod  (109+7)P \cdot Q^{-1} \mod (10^9 + 7)P⋅Q−1mod(109+7) .
The sum of NNN and the sum of MMM over all test cases won't exceed 2⋅1032 \cdot 10^32⋅103.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 :  The answers are 1/21/21/2, 3/43/43/4.
The probability of the contestants crossing 1st1^{st}1st step is 1/21/21/2 and the probability of them crossing both steps is 1/41/41/4, hence the probability of them dying before crossing 1st1^{st}1st and 2nd2^{nd}2nd steps are (1−1/2)(1 - 1/2)(1−1/2) and (1−1/4)(1 - 1/4)(1−1/4) respectively.
Test Case 2 :  The answer is 1/21/21/2
Test Case 3 :  The answers are 000, 1/41/41/4, 1/21/21/2
